name: Classic Movies
description: Golden Age Hollywood through the New Hollywood era

questions.q: 'Here's looking at you, kid' is a famous line from which classic film?

source.notes: Spoken by Humphrey Bogart to Ingrid Bergman. 1942 film, won Best Picture.

questions.q: In 'The Graduate' (1967), what one word of career advice does Mr. McGuire give to Benjamin?

source.notes: Mr. McGuire: 'I just want to say one word to you. Just one word. Plastics.' Dustin Hoffman plays Benjamin.

questions.q: Who starred as Atticus Finch in 'To Kill a Mockingbird' (1962)?

source.notes: Won Peck his only Academy Award for Best Actor.

questions.q: Who directed 'Vertigo' (1958)?

source.notes: Starred Jimmy Stewart and Kim Novak. Often ranked the greatest film ever made.

questions.q: Which actor played retired gunslinger William Munny in 'Unforgiven' (1992)?

source.notes: Eastwood also directed and produced. Won Best Picture and Best Director.

questions.q: In the 1953 Western 'High Noon,' Gary Cooper plays marshal Will Kane facing what event?

source.notes: Real-time structure (84 minutes covers 90 in-film). Won Cooper his second Best Actor Oscar.

questions.q: In 'Butch Cassidy and the Sundance Kid,' where do the outlaws flee at the film's end?

source.notes: 1969 film. Paul Newman and Robert Redford. The real Butch Cassidy reportedly died there in 1908.

questions.q: What is Clint Eastwood's character called in Sergio Leone's 'Dollars Trilogy'?

source.notes: Films: A Fistful of Dollars, For a Few Dollars More, The Good, the Bad and the Ugly.

questions.q: Who directed the 1972 film 'The Godfather'?

source.notes: Based on Mario Puzo's novel. Won Best Picture. Part II also won Best Picture.

questions.q: John Wayne won his only Best Actor Oscar for which 1969 Western?

source.notes: Played one-eyed Marshal Rooster Cogburn. Remade in 2010 with Jeff Bridges.

questions.q: In the 1960 Western 'The Magnificent Seven,' how many gunmen defend a Mexican village?

source.notes: American remake of Kurosawa's 'Seven Samurai' (1954). Iconic theme by Elmer Bernstein.

questions.q: Who played Indiana Jones in 'Raiders of the Lost Ark' (1981)?

source.notes: Selleck was the original choice but was contractually tied to 'Magnum P.I.'

questions.q: Which Stanley Kubrick film features a Nuclear War Room and characters like General Buck Turgidson?

source.notes: Full title: 'Dr. Strangelove or: How I Learned to Stop Worrying and Love the Bomb.' 1964 satire.

questions.q: In Orson Welles' 'Citizen Kane' (1941), what is the mystery word Kane whispers before dying?

source.notes: The word refers to Kane's childhood sled. Iconic opening line. Film often ranked greatest of all time.

questions.q: In 'M*A*S*H' the movie and TV series, what is the unit number of the medical unit?

source.notes: 4077th Mobile Army Surgical Hospital. Set during the Korean War.

questions.q: What song plays over the opening credits of Robert Altman's 'M*A*S*H' (1970)?

source.notes: Lyrics by Altman's 14-year-old son Mike. He earned more from royalties than Altman did from the film.

questions.q: In 'It's a Wonderful Life,' what is the name of the angel who shows George Bailey what the world would be like without him?

source.notes: Clarence Odbody, Angel Second Class. Earns his wings by saving George.

questions.q: Which director made the spaghetti western classic 'The Good, the Bad and the Ugly' (1966)?

source.notes: Italian director. Third installment of the Dollars Trilogy.

questions.q: In 'Psycho' (1960), what is the profession of Marion Crane (Janet Leigh's character)?

source.notes: She steals $40,000 from her boss before her fateful stop at the Bates Motel.

questions.q: Who played Detective Jimmy 'Popeye' Doyle in 'The French Connection' (1971)?

source.notes: Won Best Actor Oscar. Famous chase scene through Brooklyn.
